steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which statement the author would most likely disagree with. Find the author’s core claim about vocal manipulation in hyperpop, then look for the choice that contradicts it.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "It is a symbol of the influence that hyperpop exerts on listeners' sense of self.": The passage does not discuss listeners' sense of self, but this choice still treats vocal manipulation as socially meaningful rather than as a merely arbitrary aesthetic feature. It does not directly contradict the author’s core claim. ✗
  • "It represents changes to human experience brought about by technology.": The author explicitly frames vocal manipulation as commentary on how digital technology mediates human experience — this aligns with the passage. The author would not disagree. ✗
  • "It is an aesthetic feature that has little or no social import.": The passage says vocal manipulation is ‘Rather than being an arbitrary stylistic choice’ before explaining that it functions as meaningful cultural commentary — directly contradicting the claim that it has little or no social import. The author would disagree. ✓
  • "It confirms hyperpop's ability to offset certain negative effects of digital technology.": The passage does not claim vocal manipulation offsets negative effects of technology — this falls outside the author’s argument, but the author never directly contradicts it. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
